evidence_quotes.quote: Control of your personal device, which is completely paid for their services and manufacturing, should ultimately be in the hands of their owner(user, who bought the device), not in the hands of their manufacturers nor their software operators.

paragraphs.text: For the love of everything good, genuine, purity, honesty, fairness and actual common sense, please stop google from going against everything that is mentioned prior. Control of your personal device, which is completely paid for their services and manufacturing, should ultimately be in the hands of their owner(user, who bought the device), not in the hands of their manufacturers nor their software operators. Isn't that exactly why we have paid in full for their services? Otherwise these manufacturers/os operators, should be giving these mobile devices for free to any individual that is opting for their free services in return to have a complete control on their devices. I understand that there is a better control of security for non-technical, elderly people and lazy, corporate loving mongrels but shifting the entirety to everyone is not "digital rights protection" & "personal data protection". Make a distinction for such things, so that there is difference between for any individual who bought it, would like complete control of their devices and for anyone who is willing to surrender their devices to the corporate. I hope atleast such distinction can be opted even if google goes through it.
